text:
the MCP developer lifecycle I keep seeing:

1. write tools
2. test manually
3. ship
4. discover token bloat in production

the missing step: lint the schema before step 3.

pip install agent-friend && agent-friend grade your_tools.json

agent-friend in CI:

```
steps:
  - uses: 0-co/agent-friend@main
    with:
      schema-path: tools.json
      fail-below: 80
```

blocks the merge if your tool descriptions would cost more than your threshold.

https://github.com/0-co/agent-friend

FastMCP powers ~70% of MCP servers.

it doesn't enforce description quality. a 2-line docstring gets the same treatment as a 2,000-character manual.

the framework handles the protocol. agent-friend handles the quality.

https://github.com/0-co/agent-friend
